examples: Because, says a 37-liker, "It looks mysterious, like a cloaked villain from a silent movie."
Jeffrey K. Liker suggests in his new book, The Toyota Way, that this thinking is associated with traditional process improvement, focusing on local efficiencies.
Liker notes, "This is especially true because in most processes there are relatively few value-added steps, so improving those value-added steps will not amount to much."
"Somewhere the next Hyundai is out there, working from a low-cost base, " Liker says.
Liker estimates this could make a 20-30 percent difference in profits for a company like Apple.
